title: polyphragmon sericanthum
intro: Polyphragmon sericanthum, also known as "Rabbit Tobacco" or "Desert Honeysuckle", is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the respiratory system and relieve coughs. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as bronchitis, asthma, and tuberculosis, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of Polyphragmon sericanthum include smoking the leaves or infusing them in water to make a tea, which can be used to relieve respiratory symptoms and promote relaxation.
